steps.title: Identify what the blank must mean
steps.content: The passage says Martin's work "represented a significant ______ the field of chemistry" and that it contributed "the greatest benefit to mankind." The blank must describe a meaningful positive contribution to or progress within the field.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong words and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Work through each choice:
  • Choice A — exposure to: means introduction or visibility, not progress or contribution — does not match the Nobel Prize framing of benefit. ✗
  • Choice B — advancement in: means progress or improvement within the field — directly fits a Nobel-worthy contribution to chemistry. ✓
  • Choice C — dependence on: implies reliance, which carries a negative connotation and does not describe a beneficial contribution. ✗
  • Choice D — controversy in: implies dispute, not benefit — contradicts the passage's positive framing. ✗
B is correct.
